content: Just as human language learners use the lexical concept of reflexion (reflecting on writing and reading to refine communication skills) to iteratively correct linguistic mistakes, large language models can integrate explicit, human-style metacognitive reflexion routines from educational practice into their existing self-reflection mechanisms to improve task outputs. This framework frames LLM self-reflection not as a purely technical reinforcement step, but as a guided learning practice analogous to how high school students refine their writing through structured reflexion workshops.
